meta.home_desc: Product Engineer based in Vitória, ES. I build fullstack web applications, APIs and product-oriented systems — from data modeling to end-user experience.

meta.twitter_desc: Product Engineer based in Vitória, ES. Fullstack web applications, APIs and product-oriented systems.
meta.projects_desc: Project archive focused on architecture, execution, and production delivery.
meta.experience_desc: Professional and academic background of José Luiz Mendes in software engineering, public systems, and delivery quality.

hero.headline: I build complete products — from the data model to the end-user experience.
hero.sub: Robust web applications, modern interfaces and product-oriented architecture. From requirements gathering to production deploy — with end-to-end ownership.

services.title: What I build
services.lead: Specialties and areas of expertise — from backend to interface.

items.title: Fullstack Web Apps
items.desc: Next.js, React, Node.js and PostgreSQL. From database to interface, with architecture built to scale.

items.title: APIs & Integrations
items.desc: REST APIs with JWT authentication, webhooks, payments and integrations with external services.

items.title: Testing & Quality
items.desc: Unit tests, E2E with Playwright and verifiable metrics. SonarQube Grade A on critical projects.

items.title: Containers & Deploy
items.desc: Docker, Docker Compose and production deployment. CI/CD with Azure DevOps and delivery best practices.

work.title: Selected Work
work.lead: Products built end-to-end — architecture, development and delivery at production quality.

items.title: End-to-End Ownership
items.description: From database to interface, full responsibility for the product. No layer is someone else's problem.
items.signal: Entire product on the radar, no silent responsibility handoff.

items.title: Engineering for Impact
items.description: Code without impact is exercise. Every solution must solve a real product pain.
items.signal: A technical decision only holds when it reduces risk or increases outcome.

items.title: Quality as Discipline
items.description: Grade A on SonarQube isn't an accident. It's culture applied commit by commit, no exceptions.
items.signal: Quality enters the flow — not as late review or last-minute effort.

items.title: Learn in Context
items.description: Learning accelerates with real purpose. The market doesn't wait for ideal conditions.
items.signal: Learning in production builds repertoire faster than isolated study.

about.title: Product engineer, cross-disciplinary thinker
about.p1: I build digital products from zero to production — architecture, development, and delivery as a single responsibility. No intermediaries, no loss of context: every technical decision starts from understanding the client's real problem.
about.p2: Neuroscience, music, and finance aren't hobbies separate from engineering — they're lenses that change how I process a problem. That cross-disciplinary thinking shows up in data modeling, code readability, and how I communicate solutions.
about.p3: I work without shortcuts because the cost of technical debt always surfaces — in maintenance, in growth, in the next feature. When the problem is well-defined, the delivery pace shifts to a different scale.
about.motivation_text: When I decided to get married, I hadn't mastered programming yet. I used the wedding as fuel to learn. The result became the most complete project in the portfolio.

signals.title: End-to-end delivery
signals.description: From briefing to deploy: architecture, development, and operation as a single responsibility. No intermediary layers, no loss of context.

signals.title: Cross-disciplinary thinking
signals.description: Interests in neuroscience, music, and finance build a different analytical lens. Complex problems demand perspectives that go beyond the code.

signals.title: No shortcuts, no surprises
signals.description: Every decision is justified and communicated. No hidden technical debt — what works in development works exactly the same in production.

profile_cards.title: Product end-to-end, no intermediaries
profile_cards.description: Architecture, development, and delivery as a single responsibility. The client talks directly to whoever makes the technical decisions — and knows the reason behind each one.

profile_cards.title: Disciplined execution, no technical theater
profile_cards.description: Good engineering means aligning context, modeling, testing, and delivery. The solution needs to work under pressure, not just look clean in the repository.

profile_cards.title: Analytical vision beyond the code
profile_cards.description: Neuroscience, music, and finance build a cross-disciplinary reading of problems. That shows up in how I model data, communicate decisions, and prioritize what matters.

contact.availability: Available for freelance projects and opportunities at tech companies. Use the chat below or reach out directly.
contact.initial_message: Hi! I'm José's virtual assistant. How can I help you today?
contact.fallback_response: Thanks for your message! For a more detailed response, reach out directly at josemendess004@gmail.com or via LinkedIn.

quick_prompts.label: Availability
quick_prompts.prompt: What is your current availability to start a project and what is your weekly capacity?
quick_prompts.answer: I'm currently available for new projects. I can start immediately or align a date that makes sense for the scope and your urgency.

quick_prompts.label: Stack
quick_prompts.prompt: Which technologies, frameworks and tools do you master and use most frequently on projects?
quick_prompts.answer: I work primarily with React, Next.js, TypeScript, Node.js and modern frontend technologies. I also have experience with design systems, animations and product-oriented architecture.

quick_prompts.label: Budget
quick_prompts.prompt: How does your pricing model work, what are your billing options and how do you typically structure proposals?
quick_prompts.answer: Pricing is defined based on complexity and scope. I typically work with fixed project rates or hourly rates in specific cases, always with full transparency on what's included.

quick_prompts.label: Timeline
quick_prompts.prompt: What is the average delivery timeline for different types of projects, such as landing pages or full applications?
quick_prompts.answer: Landing pages usually take 1 to 2 weeks; more complex products, between 4 and 8 weeks. The focus is always balancing speed with quality and long-term maintainability.

colophon.title: Credits
colophon.lead: Visual system, stack and foundational decisions behind the interface. On mobile, the layout prioritizes short blocks and direct actions.
colophon.build_text: A more editorial colophon, with blocks grouped by function. Reduces height on mobile and improves scannability without losing density.
colophon.contact_text: Direct channels for conversation, proposals or project follow-up.

projects_page.title: Projects in continuous flow
projects_page.lead: A more editorial reading of the portfolio: two columns at opposing rhythms, with visual context before entering each case study.

experience_page.title: Professional and academic background
experience_page.lead: The history lives here as a depth layer. The home keeps selling execution, product quality, and delivery capacity.

experience_section.lead_experience: Software engineering in production, on strategic projects for the Espírito Santo State Government.
